prompt: A classical Chinese ink-wash painting on warm aged rice paper, combining landscape art and calligraphy in a clean two-part composition. On the left half, leave broad negative space filled with bold black brush-calligraphy showing the poem title 早发白帝城, the poet name 李白, and four large vertical lines of poem text in expressive handwritten script: 朝辞白帝彩云间，千里江陵一日还。两岸猿声啼不住，轻舟已过万重山。. On the right half, paint a serene river gorge at sunrise in the style of a traditional shan shui scroll: a soft orange sun low above misty layered mountains, tall steep cliffs on both sides, faint haze in the distance, and calm reflective water glowing with pale gold light. In the foreground at lower right, place a small wooden river boat with a simple covered cabin and a vertical mast. A solitary ancient Chinese scholar in flowing white hanfu stands on the deck facing the landscape from behind, hair tied in a traditional topknot, long robe and sash slightly trailing in the breeze, creating a contemplative mood. Add a tiny temple pavilion perched high on the top of the right cliff. Use delicate ink outlines, subtle sepia and gray washes, textured paper grain, elegant empty space, and refined literati-painting aesthetics. The overall image should feel poetic, quiet, balanced, and museum-quality, with especially beautiful legible calligraphy integrated into the artwork.
